title: ICE Agent Castro Arrested in Texas After Fleeing Jurisdiction
excerpt: ICE agent Christian Castro, charged with assault and falsely reporting a crime in the January 14 Minneapolis shooting, was arrested by Texas Rangers in Cameron County, Texas — 11 days after charges were filed — after leaving Minnesota. DHS called the state-level arrest 'unlawful' and a 'political stunt.'
content:
On May 30, 2026, ICE agent Christian Castro was arrested in Cameron County, Texas, 11 days after Hennepin County prosecutors charged him with assault and falsely reporting a crime in connection with the January 14, 2026 nonfatal shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis in Minneapolis.

  • Minnesota's Bureau of Criminal Apprehension located Castro in Texas, and the Texas Rangers assisted in taking him into custody in Cameron County, which borders Mexico.
  • DHS issued a statement calling the arrest 'unlawful' and a 'political stunt,' arguing the agent's conduct should be adjudicated at the federal level rather than under state law.
  • Video evidence had shown that Castro's account of the incident — that he was attacked with a shovel for three minutes — was false. The actual confrontation lasted approximately 12 seconds, and no video showed anyone attacking Castro with the shovel.
  • Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty called the arrest 'a critical step forward in our prosecution.'
  • Separately, another ICE agent, Gregory Donnell Morgan Jr., had been charged with assault for allegedly pointing his gun at people in a car on a Minneapolis highway. Morgan turned himself in and his attorney disputed the charges.
  • The case represented one of the first successful state-level criminal prosecutions of a federal ICE agent for on-duty conduct during the 2026 enforcement surge.
"In Minnesota, we believe in equal justice under the law. That means nobody is above the law, including agents of the federal government," Minnesota AG Keith Ellison said upon Castro's arrest.
